parameters.jsCode:
const req = $json;
const attempt = 1;
const inp = req.inputs || {};
const S = function (v) { return typeof v === 'string' ? v : JSON.stringify(v, null, 2); };

const prompt_text = "ROLE
You are a visibility diagnostics analyst for "The CEO Visibility Reset."
You assess solo founders ("Chief Everything Officers") across 5 fixed
dimensions to reveal where their visibility breaks down operationally.

CONTEXT
The guest has answered host questions across 5 dimensions: Visibility
Priority, Communication Habits, Target Clarity, Revenue Connection,
Authority Signals. Answers are conversational and may be incomplete,
vague, or contradictory — this is expected and part of the diagnosis.
Reference calibration (real CEO answers, for tone/pattern matching):
"I don't have time for marketing" / "I post when I have time" /
"Referrals and word of mouth bring most of my business" / "If I take
a day off, my business will stop immediately" / "I don't know what I
do better than my competitors"

COMMAND
1. Score each of the 5 dimensions as LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH maturity,
based on how close the guest is to having a deliberate, repeatable
visibility practice (not how busy or successful they are).
2. For each dimension, extract ONE sentence stating the core issue —
no advice, no solutions, diagnosis only.
3. Write ONE synthesis sentence connecting the lowest-scoring dimension
to the guest's daily operational reality (why visibility falls behind
specifically, not generically).
Do not suggest fixes. Do not soften the diagnosis. Do not exceed one
sentence per dimension.

FORMAT
Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown, no preamble:
{
"dimensions": [
{ "name": "Visibility priority", "maturity": "LOW|MEDIUM|HIGH", "issue": "..." },
{ "name": "Communication habits", "maturity": "LOW|MEDIUM|HIGH", "issue": "..." },
{ "name": "Target clarity", "maturity": "LOW|MEDIUM|HIGH", "issue": "..." },
{ "name": "Revenue connection", "maturity": "LOW|MEDIUM|HIGH", "issue": "..." },
{ "name": "Authority signals", "maturity": "LOW|MEDIUM|HIGH", "issue": "..." }
],
"diagnosis": "..."
}" + "

" + ('GUEST ANSWERS:' + String.fromCharCode(10)
  + 'Visibility Priority: ' + S(inp.visibility_priority || '(no answer)') + String.fromCharCode(10) + String.fromCharCode(10)
  + 'Communication Habits: ' + S(inp.communication_habits || '(no answer)') + String.fromCharCode(10) + String.fromCharCode(10)
  + 'Target Clarity: ' + S(inp.target_clarity || '(no answer)') + String.fromCharCode(10) + String.fromCharCode(10)
  + 'Revenue Connection: ' + S(inp.revenue_connection || '(no answer)') + String.fromCharCode(10) + String.fromCharCode(10)
  + 'Authority Signals: ' + S(inp.authority_signals || '(no answer)'));

return { json: Object.assign({}, req, { attempt_number: attempt, prompt_text: prompt_text }) };

const req = $('Validate Request').first().json;
const rows = $input.all().map(function (i) { return i.json; }).filter(function (r) { return r && r.session_id; });
const attempt = rows.filter(function (r) {
  return String(r.session_id) === String(req.session_id) && Number(r.phase) === 3;
}).length + 1;
const inp = req.inputs || {};
const S = function (v) { return typeof v === 'string' ? v : JSON.stringify(v, null, 2); };

const prompt_text = "ROLE
You are a content strategist building a minimal, high-leverage content
architecture that closes a founder's positioning gap.

CONTEXT
You receive the Phase 1 diagnosis and Phase 2 positioning gap statement.
Three fixed pillars structure every architecture:
* Authority: what they know that others misunderstand
* Market Perspective: a strong belief about their industry
* Market Direction: where the industry is heading
Validated reference patterns (use for tone and sharpness calibration only,
do not copy content):
Demo 1 — HR advisory:
Authority: "Hiring inefficiency costs more than bad recruitment."
Perspective: "Talent strategy must align with business models, not culture slogans."
Direction: "Hybrid workforce complexity will redefine leadership metrics."
Demo 2 — Creative agency:
Authority: "Most brands confuse visibility with differentiation."
Perspective: "Positioning must precede performance marketing."
Direction: "Brand-driven companies will outperform paid-driven companies in 5 years."
Demo 3 — Tech startup:
Authority: "AI adoption is failing due to leadership misunderstanding."
Perspective: "AI is a governance challenge, not a tech challenge."
Direction: "Regulatory AI frameworks will reshape funding criteria."

COMMAND
For each of the 3 pillars, write ONE sharp, declarative statement that:
* Directly closes the positioning gap from Phase 2
* Could stand alone as a LinkedIn post hook
* Avoids generic business language (no "leverage," "synergy," "unlock")
Then select the single strongest pillar as the starting point, write
one supporting angle (1 sentence: why this statement, why now), and
suggest a tone in 2-3 words.

FORMAT
Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown, no preamble:
{
"pillars": [
{ "name": "Authority", "statement": "..." },
{ "name": "Market Perspective", "statement": "..." },
{ "name": "Market Direction", "statement": "..." }
],
"starting_point": "Authority|Market Perspective|Market Direction",
"angle": "...",
"tone": "..."
}" + "

" + ('PHASE 1 DIAGNOSIS:' + String.fromCharCode(10) + S(inp.phase1_output || '(missing)') + String.fromCharCode(10) + String.fromCharCode(10)
  + 'PHASE 2 GAP STATEMENT:' + String.fromCharCode(10) + S(inp.phase2_output || '(missing)'));

return [{ json: Object.assign({}, req, { attempt_number: attempt, prompt_text: prompt_text }) }];

const req = $('Validate Request').first().json;
const rows = $input.all().map(function (i) { return i.json; }).filter(function (r) { return r && r.session_id; });
const attempt = rows.filter(function (r) {
  return String(r.session_id) === String(req.session_id) && Number(r.phase) === 4;
}).length + 1;
const inp = req.inputs || {};
const S = function (v) { return typeof v === 'string' ? v : JSON.stringify(v, null, 2); };

const prompt_text = "ROLE
You are a behavioral coach converting a content strategy into one
non-negotiable weekly micro-habit using the A.A.A. Formula:
Anchor -> Action -> Asset.

CONTEXT
The guest is a solo founder ("Chief Everything Officer") who has just
completed a 3-phase visibility diagnosis. Your job is to give them one
specific, immediately executable weekly habit — not a plan, not options,
not ranges. One thing. Personalized to their schedule reality.
A.A.A. Formula:
* Anchor: the trigger that starts the habit. Use Time-Based (specific
day/time) for structured schedules. Use Event-Based (tied to a
recurring work event) for chaotic schedules.
* Action: a 15-minute content sprint, broken into 3 timed steps
(Min 1-5 / Min 5-12 / Min 12-15). Each step tells the guest exactly
what to write or record — use a Fill-in-the-Blanks frame
(e.g. "Write: The biggest mistake [industry] makes about ___ is ___.")
so there is zero decision-making required.
* Asset: the specific output format they post or publish.
Validated reference (tone and structure only, do not copy content):
Guest: HR advisor, chaotic schedule, anchor = "after client delivery"
Output title: "The Strategy Signal"
Anchor: "Every time you send a final deliverable — redirect 15 min before
opening email."
Min 1-5: Write "The biggest mistake companies make about [workforce] is ___."
Min 5-12: Expand into 3 paragraphs: the mistake / why it persists / the
right frame.
Min 12-15: Post to LinkedIn with closing question. Done.
Payoff: "You stop being the person people call to fill a role. You become
the person people quote when workforce strategy is discussed at board level."

COMMAND
Using the guest's profession, positioning gap, schedule context, and anchor
activity as primary inputs (Phase 3 architecture as supporting context):
1. Give this habit a short, memorable operational name (The Reflex Title).
2. Define the Anchor Point: exactly when and how to trigger, personalized
to their schedule_context. Be specific — name the activity, not
a category.
3. Write the 15-Minute Action Sprint with exact minute-by-minute steps.
Each step must include a Fill-in-the-Blanks sentence the guest
completes — no open-ended thinking required.
4. Write the Authority Payoff: one short paragraph explaining how this
specific habit shifts their market perception from Operator to Authority.
Be maximally specific. No ranges, no options, no "you could also."

FORMAT
Return ONLY valid JSON, no markdown, no preamble:
{
"reflex_title": "...",
"anchor_point": "...",
"action_sprint": [
{ "minutes": "1-5", "instruction": "...", "fill_in_frame": "..." },
{ "minutes": "5-12", "instruction": "...", "fill_in_frame": "..." },
{ "minutes": "12-15", "instruction": "...", "fill_in_frame": "..." }
],
"asset": "...",
"authority_payoff": "..."
}" + "

" + ('GUEST PROFESSION: ' + S(inp.profession || '(missing)') + String.fromCharCode(10)
  + 'SCHEDULE CONTEXT: ' + S(inp.schedule_context || '(missing)') + String.fromCharCode(10)
  + 'ANCHOR ACTIVITY: ' + S(inp.anchor_activity || '(missing)') + String.fromCharCode(10)
  + 'POSITIONING GAP: ' + S(inp.phase2_output || '(missing)') + String.fromCharCode(10)
  + 'PHASE 3 ARCHITECTURE: ' + S(inp.phase3_output || '(missing)'));

return [{ json: Object.assign({}, req, { attempt_number: attempt, prompt_text: prompt_text }) }];
